name: Diabetic Orange Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons granulated sugar replacement (or granulated fructose)
recipeInstructions:
1. Juice the orange and remove the seeds from the juice.
2. Cream together the shortening and sugar replacement (or fructose). Add the egg, orange juice, and grated orange rind, beating until light and fluffy.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a sifter. Add the dry ingredients alternately with buttermilk to the creamed mixture, stirring until well blended.
4. Drop spoonfuls of dough onto lightly greased cookie sheets, spacing them 2-3 inches apart.
5. Bake at 375°F for 10-12 minutes.
Note:
- 1 cookie with sugar replacement: 1/3 fruit, 1/3 fat Calories: 27
- 1 cookie made with fructose: 1/3 fruit, 1/3 fat Calories: 30
Source: Diabetic Candy, Cookie and Dessert Cookbook by MaryJane Finsand.
